channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Abortion opponents shift focus to pills with lawsuits, proposed laws and possible federal action
item.description: The battles over abortion in the U.S. are increasingly focusing on the pills that are now the most common way pregnancies are ended.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 49ers eliminated from NFC playoffs before game against Miami. Cowboys, Saints, Cardinals also out
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — The defending NFC champion San Francisco 49ers were eliminated from the playoffs before their game at Miami began on Sunday because of victories by Washington and the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police announced Sunday they have in custody a “person of interest” in the early morning death of a woman who they believe may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being intentionally lit on fire by a man she didn't know.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 174 Colorado skiers and snowboarders rescued after a lift cracks
item.description: WINTER PARK, Colo. (AP) — Officials were investigating Sunday what caused a crack in a Colorado ski lift that forced the evacuation of over 170 stranded skiers and snowboarders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The highest-ranking uniformed officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate in exchange for opportunities to earn extra pay.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: UPDATE: Missing 63-year-old man from Pueblo located
item.description: UPDATE: SATURDAY 12/21/2024 6:38 p.m. (PUEBLO, Colo.) — PPD reported that Palacio has been located and thanks the community for their help. ORIGINAL HEADLINE: PPD requests help finding missing 63-year-old man SATURDAY 12/21/2024 4:53 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Dara Korn, NewsNation

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KRON4

item.title: Texans WR Tank Dell hospitalized after severe knee injury on TD catch vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans wide receiver Tank Dell was taken to the University of Kansas Medical Center and the team said he would remain overnight after sustaining a severe left knee injury while making a 30-yard touchdown catch in a 27-19 loss to the Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: It's eggnog season. The boozy beverage dates back to medieval England but remains a holiday hit
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — At Scoma’s Restaurant in San Francisco, this holiday season 's batch of eggnog began 11 months ago. The process typically starts in late January, just after the previous year's celebrations are over.
item.dc:creator: TERRY CHEA, STEPHEN SMITH and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Party City stores will stay open despite national closures — here's where
item.description: Party City stores in Hawaii will remain open, even as the national chain announced plans to close 850 locations across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Lopez, KRON4

item.title: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the Yankees agree to a $12.5 million, 1-year deal, AP source says
item.description: First baseman Paul Goldschmidt and the New York Yankees agreed Saturday to a $12.5 million, one-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — A rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv overnight, leaving 16 people injured by shattered glass, the Israeli military said Saturday, days after Israeli airstrikes hit Houthi rebels who have been launching missiles in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, Fox 5

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old's butt at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Here's a look at the $100 billion in disaster relief in the government spending bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress is allocating more than $100 billion in emergency aid designed to address extensive damage caused by disasters after this week's scramble to find consensus on a government spending bill.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) marked the achievement after the Senate confirmed Serena Raquel Murillo to serve as district judge for the Central District of California.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 5 of the most frustrating health insurer tactics and why they exist
item.description: Even for those with health insurance, coverage does not ensure access to care, much less high-quality and affordable care.
item.dc:creator: Monica S. Aswani Assistant Professor of Health Services Administration, University of Alabama at Birmingham, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Santa and Mrs. Claus use military transports to bring Christmas to an Alaska Native village
item.description: YAKUTAT, Alaska (AP) — Forget the open-air sleigh overloaded with gifts and powered by flying reindeer. Santa and Mrs.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada election leaders find nearly 200 cases of double votes from November
item.description: The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office is investigating 180 double votes from the 2024 general election, a report released Friday indicated.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin police chief overseeing school shooting probe is named to Seattle job
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Madison Police Chief Shon Barnes was named as the chief of police in Seattle on Friday, five days after he was thrust into the national spotlight because of a shooting at a religious school in Wisconsin that killed a teacher and student.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER and TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
